title: Gotu kola infusion for mental clarity
subtitle: A nerve-calming herb infusion for focus and cognitive tiredness
excerpt: An infusion of dried gotu kola leaf. Traditionally used in Ayurveda and western herbal practice for mental fatigue, difficulty concentrating, and cognitive tiredness. Calming without being sedating.
sourceNotes: Ayurvedic Pharmacopoeia of India, Centella asiatica reference entry: the medhya rasayana (cognitive tonic) tradition. the World Health Organisation Monographs on Selected Medicinal Plants, Vol. 1, Herba Centellae. the European herbal standard assessment on Centellae asiaticae herba, the review of traditional use for cognitive support.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Dried gotu kola stores 12 months in a sealed tin, cool and dark. Prepare each cup fresh.

glossaryTerms.definition: An Ayurvedic category of herbs traditionally described as cognitive tonics that support memory, clarity, and mental resilience. Gotu kola is one of the four canonical medhya rasayana herbs.

content.text: Gotu kola (Centella asiatica) is one of the traditional cognitive-tonic herbs in the Ayurvedic system.

herbs. Western herbalists class it as a nerve-calming and stress-supporting herb. The infusion is mild and slightly bitter. A morning cup is the traditional approach for focus and mental clarity. The herb is calming without causing drowsiness.

attrs.title: Important safety notes
attrs.body:
Not medical advice. Consult a qualified herbalist or doctor for ongoing or serious symptoms.

Pregnancy. Gotu kola is avoided at therapeutic doses in pregnancy as a precaution, as it has mild uterine activity noted in some studies. Consult your midwife before any therapeutic use in pregnancy.

Liver. Isolated cases of liver harm have been reported with high-dose gotu kola supplement products. Standard infusion doses (1-2 g dried herb per cup, 1-2 cups per day) are within the safe range per the World Health Organisation reference entry, but prolonged use (more than 3 months continuously) should be discussed with a herbalist. Readers with existing liver conditions should consult their doctor.

Cognitive symptoms that need medical attention. Significant, progressive, or rapidly worsening difficulty with memory or concentration should be medically assessed. Gotu kola is appropriate for mild cognitive tiredness and everyday difficulty focusing, not for significant cognitive change.

items.prepNote: dried Centella asiatica leaf, about 1 teaspoon

content.text: Place the dried gotu kola in a covered teapot. Pour over the just-boiled water. Put the lid on and steep for 8 minutes. The infusion will be pale gold and mildly herbal. Strain into a cup. Drink warm, ideally in the morning.

One or two cups per day, in the morning or mid-morning. the World Health Organisation reference entry reference dose is 0.3-0.6 g dried herb three times daily (approximately 1-2 g per cup at this preparation's dose is within range). Take for up to 3 months continuously; take a 4-week break before another course. Paediatric use: not for children under twelve without specialist herbalist advice.
Prepare each cup fresh. Dried gotu kola stores 12 months in a sealed tin, cool and dark.
When not to use this remedy
In pregnancy. With liver problems without a doctor's guidance. Serious memory issues need a doctor.
